[[Wes Naiqama]] (brother) }} }} '''Kevin Naiqama''' (born 4 February 1989) is a [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] international former [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Western Suburbs Rosellas]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]] and as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|fe}} for [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] at the International level. He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]], [[Penrith Panthers]], [[Wests Tigers]] and the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL), and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Naiqama was born in [[Sutherland, New South Wales]], Australia{{cn|date=June 2024}}, and is of [[Fijians|Fijian]] descent. Naiqama is the younger brother of former [[London Broncos]] player and Fijian international, [[Wes Naiqama]].{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=29925&young-gun-gets-first-run|title=YOUNG GUN GETS FIRST RUN|publisher=Newcastle Knights|date=2010-09-03|access-date=2010-09-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927141438/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=29925&young-gun-gets-first-run|archive-date=2011-09-27}} ==Playing career== ===Early career=== After playing with the St. George Illawarra Dragons, Naiqama joined the [[Newcastle Knights]] alongside his brother Wes in 2008. He played for the Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2008 and 2009, scoring 21 tries in 33 games.{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/n/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150624123605/http://nycdatabase.org/players/n/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=24 June 2015|title=N|date=27 December 2012|publisher=Nycdatabase.org|access-date=2015-06-26}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/n/naiqama_kevin/index.php|publisher=Rleague.com|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120405000645/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/n/naiqama_kevin/index.php|title=Kevin Naiqama|archive-date=5 April 2012|access-date=2015-05-02}} [[File:Kevin Naiqama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Naiqama playing for the Central Coast Centurions in 2011]] ===2009=== Naiqama played for [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in the [[2009 Pacific Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/2009-pacific-cup-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/57082/default.aspx|title=2009 Pacific Cup: Squads|publisher=NRL.com|date=2009-10-10|access-date=2015-05-02}} ===2010=== Moving to the Knights' [[New South Wales Cup]] team [[Central Coast Centurions]], Naiqama played for the [[New South Wales Residents rugby league team|New South Wales Residents]] against the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] at [[Stadium Australia|ANZ Stadium]] as a curtain raiser to Game three of the [[2010 State of Origin series|State of Origin series]].{{cite web|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/updated-new-south-wales-residents-team?redo=1|title=Updated: New South Wales Residents team|publisher=League Unlimited|date=2010-06-28|access-date=2015-05-02}} In Round 26, Naiqama made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Knights against the [[Melbourne Storm]] on the wing in the 34–4 loss at [[AAMI Park]].{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/storm-win-last-game-of-pointless-season/story-e6frep5x-1225914434167?nk=be0dfabf75e2ad5c52311115b306a4d2|title=Storm Win Last Game of Pointless Season|work=The Courier-Mail|access-date=2015-06-26}} At the end of 2010, Naiqama was named on the wing in the New South Wales Cup Team of the Year.{{cite web|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/pita-godinet-named-in-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year|title=Pita Godinet named in NSW Cup Team of the Year|publisher=League Unlimited|date=2010-09-09|access-date=2015-05-02}} ===2011=== In June, Naiqama re-signed with the Knights on a 1-year contract,{{cite web|last=Dillon|first=Robert|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/472255/naiqama-brothers-extend-stay-with-knights/|title=Naiqama brothers extend stay with Knights | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2011-06-29|access-date=2015-05-02}} but did not make an appearance for the Knights in the [[2011 NRL season|2011 season]]. ===2012=== On 3 August, Naiqama re-signed with the Knights on a 1-year contract.{{cite web| work=newcastleknights.com.au| title=Knights re-sign Kevin Naiqama| url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/league/benjis-care-tactics/2005/09/30/1127804661003.html|access-date=23 April 2011|date=1 October 2005|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131019122757/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights-resign-Kevin-Naiqama/60838|archive-date=19 October 2013 }} In Round 17 against the [[Parramatta Eels]] at [[Parramatta Stadium]], he scored his first NRL try in the Knights' 20–12 win.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/newcastle-knights-punish-parramatta-eels-for-a-sloppy-second-half-for-a-tense-20-12-victory/story-fn2mcuj6-1226413221829?nk=be0dfabf75e2ad5c52311115b306a4d2|title=Newcastle Knights Punish Parramatta Eels for a Sloppy Second Half|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-06-26}} [[File:KEVIN NAIQAMA.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Naiqama playing for the Newcastle Knights in 2012]] He finished the [[2012 NRL season|season]] with 8 matches and 7 tries. ===2013=== On 21 June, Naiqama signed a 2-year contract with the [[Penrith Panthers]] starting in 2014, to join his older brother Wes. Knights coach [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] told Naiqama to move clubs and further his career as he was behind representative players [[Darius Boyd]], [[James McManus (rugby league)|James McManus]] and [[Akuila Uate]] at the Knights.{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Naiqama-signs-with-Panthers-for-2014/77683|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131019121141/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Naiqama-signs-with-Panthers-for-2014/77683|archive-date=2013-10-19|title=Naiqama signs with Panthers for 2014}}{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/panthers-panthers-sign-kevin-naiqama-on-two-year-deal-to-join-older-brother-wes/story-fn2mcuj6-1226667653387|title=Panthers Sign Kevin Naiqama on Two Year Deal to Join Older Brother Wes|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-06-26}} He finished the [[2013 NRL season|2013 season]] having played in 6 matches and scoring 2 tries. At the end of the season, he played for Fiji at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]],{{cite web|url=http://fijilive.com/sports/news/2013/09/civoniceva-wes-naiqama-to-lead-fiji-bati/32752.Fijilive |title=Civoniceva, Wes Naiqama to lead Fiji Bati |publisher=Fijilive.com |date=2013-09-19 |access-date=2015-05-02 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304054057/http://fijilive.com/sports/news/2013/09/civoniceva-wes-naiqama-to-lead-fiji-bati/32752.Fijilive |archive-date= 4 March 2016 }} playing in 5 matches and scoring 1 try. ===2014=== In February, Naiqama played for the Panthers at the inaugural [[2014 Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]].{{cite web|author=John Dean|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|work=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|access-date=2015-05-02|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=15 January 2016}} In Round 2 against the Melbourne Storm, he made his Panthers debut, scoring a double in the 18–17 loss.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-melbourne-storm-have-beaten-the-penrith-panthers-1817-in-a-thriller-at-aami-park/story-e6frf3sc-1226855746861?nk=1ce117dc986fb2c2dfd45b94061beb04|title=Melbourne Storm Have Beaten the Penrith Panthers 18–17 in a Thriller|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-06-26}} On 3 May, he played for Fiji in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations#Samoa v. Fiji|2014 Pacific Test]], scoring a try in the 32–16 loss.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/gutsy-samoa-secures-place-in-four-nations-tournament-following-win-over-fiji/story-fni3fbgz-1226904268873|title=Gutsy Samoa secures place in Four Nations tournament|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Australia|access-date=2015-06-26}} [[File:Kevin Naiqama Hercules 2014.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Naiqama at the premiere of [[Hercules (2014 film)|Hercules]] in 2014]] [[File:Kevin Naiqama Penrith.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Naiqama playing for the Penrith Panthers in 2014]] He finished off the [[2014 NRL season|NRL season]] with 8 matches and 7 tries. On 10 October 2014, Naiqama signed a 1-year contract with the [[Wests Tigers]] starting in 2015.{{cite web|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2014/10/10/wests_tigers_sign_backline_duo.html|title=Wests Tigers sign backline duo|publisher=Weststigers.com.au|access-date=2015-05-02}}{{cite web|author=John Dean|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/wests-tigers-sign-backline-duo/|title=Wests Tigers Sign Backline Duo|work=Rugby League Week|date=2014-10-10|access-date=2015-05-02|archive-date=5 February 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150205051119/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/wests-tigers-sign-backline-duo/|url-status=dead}} ===2015=== Naiqama made his first appearance for the Wests Tigers in the [[2015 Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]]. After what the [[Sydney Morning Herald]] called an "impressive showing", he said, "I'm the fittest and fastest I have felt in a couple of years, having done my first full pre-season in two years."{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald| title=Auckland Nines in brief: Naiqama impresses in Wests Tigers debut| url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/auckland-nines-in-brief-naiqama-impresses-in-wests-tigers-debut-20150201-133epu.html|access-date=3 May 2015|date=1 February 2015}} With regular winger [[David Nofoaluma]] recovering from injury,{{cite web| work= nrl.com| title=Smashing roadblocks Naiqama's Tigers priority| first = Jack| last = Brady| url=https://www.nrl.com/smashing-roadblocks-naiqamas-tigers-priority/tabid/10874/newsid/83533/default.aspx|access-date=3 May 2015|date=28 January 2015}} Naiqama was named in the starting side for the opening games of the season. In round 1 against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], Naiqama made his debut for the Wests Tigers in the 19–18 loss at [[Robina Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/strong-crowd-watches-gold-coast-titans-suffer-heartbreaking-onepoint-loss-to-wests-tigers/news-story/b1ab3afd5de2bc0824d620ca63d8f286|title=Tigers field goal sinks Titans' hopes}} In Round 2 against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], Naiqama scored his first try for the Tigers in the 22–4 win at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/dragons/tigers-v-dragons-james-tedesco-puts-in-five-star-performance-as-tigers-dominate-dragons/news-story/346d095233eafe3c519e0ce4d7b7137a|title=Shining star Tedesco puts Dragons to sword|date=10 March 2015}} On 15 April 2015, Naiqama signed a two-year extension with the club.{{cite web| work=Zero Tackle| title=WESTS TIGERS RE-SIGN KEVIN NAIQAMA| url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/wests-tigers-re-sign-kevin-naiqama-17106/| access-date=3 May 2015| date=16 April 2015| archive-date=19 April 2015| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150419173751/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/wests-tigers-re-sign-kevin-naiqama-17106/| url-status=dead}} In round 8 against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], Naiqama did one of the best try assists of the year, when he miraculously flicked a ball, that looked go over the dead ball line, back to James Tedesco. When asked about his try assist being similar to Greg Inglis's in the [[2008 ANZAC Test]], Naiqama said "I think his was more acrobatic than mine. But it was just real lucky to pull it off and get the try in the end".{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/bulldogs-v-tigers-kevin-naiqama-pulls-off-amazing-try-assist-at-anz-stadium/news-story/fe21c4d8573f086ee357fa09bc5ec0c3|title=Video: Fresh Prince's freakish try assist}} On 2 May, Naiqama played at fullback for Fiji against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in the [[International rugby league in 2015#Melanesian Cup|2015 Melanesian Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/marika-koroibete-stars-as-fiji-prove-too-strong-for-png-kumuls-20150502-1myjhl.html|title=Marika Koroibete stars as Fiji prove too strong for PNG Kumuls|first=Chris|last=Barrett|date=2 May 2015|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}} From Round 21 onwards, when [[David Nofoaluma]] returned from injury, Naiqama was shifted from the wing to centre. Naiqama finished his [[2015 NRL season|first year with the Wests Tigers]] with 9 tries from 24 matches. ===2016=== Naiqama was named in the Tigers [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]] squad.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/2016-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-official-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/91563/default.aspx|title=2016 Downer NRL Auckland Nines Official Squads}} Remaining at right centre in 2016, Naiqama scored his first treble in the Round 3 match against the Titans in the 30–18 loss at Robina Stadium.{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/nrl-2016-gold-coast-titans-send-wests-tigers-crashing-back-to-earth-with-round-three-victory/news-story/1bd44c2fafcc431d9cbf771073e9148c|title=Titans send Tigers crashing back to earth}} By mid-season, he was named as one of the most improved players of the year, and it was said he had, "become a genuinely dangerous player down the Tigers' right side, appearing in every game this season. His success is the result of nothing but hard work and persistence."{{cite web| work= nrl.com| title=Voss: Who is most improved in 2016?| author=Andrew Voss| url=https://www.nrl.com/voss-who-is-most-improved-in-2016/tabid/10874/newsid/98093/default.aspx}} Naiqama finished the [[2016 NRL season|season]] with 11 tries from 23 matches. On 8 October, Naiqama represented Fiji in a [[2016 Samoa vs. Fiji rugby league test match|test]] against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in [[Apia]], captaining the team to a 20–18 victory.{{cite web| work= Fox Sports| title=Match Centre| url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/match?matchid=RLI20160201}} ===2017=== In February, Naiqama played in the [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/2017-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/103308/default.aspx|title=2017 Downer NRL Auckland Nines squads}} On 6 May, Naiqama again played as captain and fullback for Fiji against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the [[2017 Pacific Rugby League Tests|2017 Pacific Cup]]. He set up a try for [[Ben Nakubuwai]] just before half time in the thrilling match but they lost 26–24 in the dying minutes.{{cite web|url=http://wwos.nine.com.au/2017/05/06/19/50/tonga-break-fiji-s-hearts-in-test-thriller|title=Late Latu try grabs Test win for Tonga – NRL}} Naiqama made 23 appearances for Wests Tigers, playing centre and wing, and covering fullback when [[James Tedesco]] was injured. At season's end, he signed an extension to stay at the club for another year. Naiqama noted he was "stoked" to be staying, and coach [[Ivan Cleary]] said, "Kevin has shown great versatility for the team this year covering a number of positions and has handled himself exceptionally well."{{cite web| work= weststigers.com | title=Kevin Naiqama commits to Wests Tigers| url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/08/29/wests_tigers_re_sign.html|archive-date=19 November 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171119031320/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/08/29/wests_tigers_re_sign.html}} Playing in his second World Cup, but his first as captain of Fiji, Naiqama gained attention when he was seen crying during his team's national anthem before the first game.{{cite web| work= Zero Tackle | title=World Cup Pre-Match almost as good as the game itself| author=Dan Nicholls | url=https://www.zerotackle.com/world-cup-pre-match-almost-good-game-30089/|archive-date=19 November 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171119035852/https://www.zerotackle.com/world-cup-pre-match-almost-good-game-30089/}} He scored two tries in that match against [[United States national rugby league team|the USA]], and another in the match against [[Italy national rugby league team results|Italy]] for three tries from five games. Coach [[Michael Potter|Mick Potter]] said of his captaincy, "The position he plays is good for him to be captain. He can boss a few people around but he also shows composure under pressure. I think that's really important. He speaks well, he speaks rugby league. He doesn't talk rubbish and he gets to the point very quickly."{{cite web| work= nrl.com | title=Naiqama's captaincy inspires Fiji to greatness| author=Eden Richards | url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/11/09/naiqamas-captaincy-inspires-fiji-to-greatness/}} ===2018=== Naiqama made 20 appearances for Wests in the [[2018 NRL season]] as the club finished in 9th place on the table and missed out on the finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/06/wests-tigers-2018-season-review/|title=Wests Tigers 2018 season review|website=NRL}} On 22 May 2018 Naiqama signed a three-year contract with St Helens. ===2019=== After his contract ran out with the [[Wests Tigers]], Naiqama joined English side St Helens. He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4–18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|work=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}} In his first season with St Helens, the club won the [[League Leaders Shield]] after finishing 16 points ahead of second placed Wigan. Naiqama then played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] which St Helens won 23–6 at [[Old Trafford]] securing the club's 14th championship.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23–6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian}}{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|work=The Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}} ===2020=== In the 2020 semi-final against [[Catalans Dragons]], Naiqama scored a [[hat-trick]] in St Helens 48–2 victory at [[Langtree Park]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2020/nov/20/st-helens-catalans-dragons-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens demolish Catalans Dragons to set up Grand Final clash with Wigan|website=www.theguardian.com}} He played in the club's 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ===2021=== In round 3 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries for St. Helens in a 34–6 victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/56734710.amp/|title=Super League: St Helens 34-6 Wakefield Trinity - Saints steamroller their way to victory|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 17 July, he played for St. Helens in their 26-12 [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}} Naiqama's final game for St Helens was the [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] where Saints won a close match 12–10 over the [[Catalans Dragons]]. Naiqama scored two tries in the final and was awarded the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] as man of the match.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 8 December, he joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] for the [[2022 NRL season]]. ===2022=== In round 4 of the [[2022 NRL season]], he made his club debut for the [[Sydney Roosters]] scoring a try in their 28–4 victory over North Queensland.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/watch-kevin-naiqama-scores-roosters-try-nrl-return/|title=Kevin Naiqama scores try in Roosters debut|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} On 21 June 2022, it was reported that Naiqama would sign a two-year deal with the [[Huddersfield Giants]].{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62259661|title=Kevin Naiqama: Huddersfield Giants to sign ex-St Helens centre in 2023|date=21 July 2022|publisher=BBC Sport}} Naiqama played in all four matches at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] for Fiji including the quarter-final where he scored two tries in their controversial 24–18 loss to New Zealand.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/new-zealand-vs-fiji/58150#:~:text=Jordan%20Rapana%20came%20to%20New,final%20against%20defending%20champions%20Australia./|title=New Zealand 24-18 Fiji: Jordan Rapana scores late try to set up semi-final date against Australia|website=www.skysports.com}} ===2023=== Naiqama played 25 matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season and scored 14 tries as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com}} === 2024 === Naiqama was one of many players during the NRL Grand Final who were given a farewell to the 2024 retiring players.{{Cite web |date=2024-10-06 |title=NRL to honour retiring players |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/10/06/nrl-to-honour-retiring-players/ |access-date=2024-10-06
